senses.gloss: Lord; Saint; Mar (honorific title for a man of religion, most often a saint)

examples.text: لَيْسَ كُلُّ مَنْ يَقُولُ لِي: يَا مَار! يَا مَار! يَدْخُلُ مَلَكُوتَ السَّمَاوَاتِ، بَلْ مَنْ يَعْمَلُ بِإِرَادَةِ أَبِي الَّذِي فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ
examples.english: Not everyone who says to me, O Lord! O Lord! will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
the above-mentioned, the aforesaid, the above

senses.gloss: going by, walking past, riding past, going across, walking, transient
to budge, to move forth, to rise across the surface, to fluctuate, to undergo commotion

examples.text: ولا تحدث الأعاصير في العادة بشكل متزامن في المحيطين، فحين يمور المحيط الهادئ بالعاصفة يكون المحيط الأطلسي هادئا".
examples.english: Typhoons don’t usually appear simultaneously in the two oceans: When the Pacific Ocean is moved by a storm the Atlantic Ocean is calm.

etymology.text:
From Classical Syriac ܡܪܝ (mār(ī)), the first-person singular possessed form of ܡܪܐ (mārā, “lord, master”).

Derived from the active participle of مَرَّ (marra, “to pass, to stroll by”).
